title: FiRe 2026: AI Meets the University
snippet: With each passing day, AI tools are changing the nature of academia. Join us for a discussion of the complex opportunities AI presents to universities, researchers, and students; and of the ongoing endeavor to better perfect human knowledge and understanding. Speakers: Pradeep Khosla, Chancellor, UCSD.
match_reason: social (video) — Pradeep Khosla is the featured speaker in this YouTube video discussing the impact of AI on higher education.

title: Statement from UC Academic Senate on admissions review
snippet: The Academic Senate is not rescinding its commitment to a comprehensive review of standardized testing in admissions. Recognizing the significance of this issue, the Academic Senate is revising its timeline while ensuring the forthcoming review is thorough, evidence-based and informed by faculty expertise.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Ahmet Palazoglu is the University of California Academic Senate Chair who issued this official statement regarding the review of admissions policies, which are being reevaluated in part due to the widespread adoption of AI.

title: Assembly Committee Adopts Resolution Urging Intersegmental Dialogue on AI in Higher Education
snippet: The Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted SCR 82, a resolution urging California's public higher education segments—the UC, CSU, and California Community Colleges—to convene a joint workgroup to coordinate on best practices for AI in instruction, student privacy, and campus services.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This legislative development directly impacts UC systemwide AI policy and mandates intersegmental collaboration on academic integrity, teaching, and privacy.

title: At the CSU, We See Access to Technology as an Equity Issue
snippet: In an op-ed, California State University leadership discusses their systemwide AI strategy to prevent an 'AI digital divide.' Highlighting a survey of 94,000 respondents, they argue that limiting access deepens inequity and makes it harder to teach the responsible and ethical use of AI.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — This item addresses AI access, equity, and systemwide strategy at a peer California public university system, which is highly relevant to the committee's charge.

title: CSU's AI Strategy: Preventing a Digital Divide Through Equitable Access
snippet: In an op-ed detailing the California State University's systemwide AI strategy, leaders argue that access to technology is a fundamental equity issue. By providing free, structured access to AI tools, the CSU aims to prevent an 'AI digital divide' where low-income and underrepresented students are left to navigate and pay for advanced technology on their own.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — This piece directly addresses the committee's focus on AI access, equity, and the digital divide for underrepresented students within California's public university systems.

title: CSU Launches 'AI in July 2026' Educational and Showcase Series
snippet: The California State University launched 'AI in July 2026,' a month-long series of online sessions, panel discussions, and campus showcases highlighting AI initiatives across its 22 universities. The series focuses on ethical AI integration, workforce preparation, and practical workshops to establish baseline AI literacy for university staff and faculty.
match_reason: websearch (other) — This systemwide initiative demonstrates how a peer California institution is operationalizing AI literacy, professional development, and ethical training at scale.

title: WARP-RM: A Warp-Augmented Relative Progress Reward Model for Data Curation
snippet: Scaling imitation learning requires large datasets, yet human teleoperation inevitably produces mixed-quality demonstrations containing hesitations and recoveries. Prior frame-level progress reward models supervise on absolute temporal progress proxies that suffer from label noise, or require costly human annotations to define subtask boundaries. We present WARP (Warp-Augmented Relative Progress),

title: T-Rex: Tactile-Reactive Dexterous Manipulation
snippet: The ability to react dynamically to tactile signals has long been considered crucial to agile human-level dexterity. title: “Gender, Power, and AI”: How gender bias, male-centered mythologies, and resource extraction reproduce and amplify inequalities
snippet: Noble, author of the groundbreaking book Algorithms of Oppression, turned to Black feminism and transnational feminism frameworks to help contextualize the larger costs of these choices, both in terms of extraction and waste and in the effects of the technologies themselves on users.

title: Webinar: Implications of AI for Cybersecurity
snippet: On June 24, the National Academies will release Implications of Recent Advancements in Artificial Intelligence for Cybersecurity, a rapid expert consultation that examines how recent advances in generative AI and agentic systems are reshaping the cybersecurity landscape. Director Giovanni Vigna contributed to the material supporting this webinar.

title: AI Will Elevate Near-Term Cybersecurity Risks but — with Investment and Coordination — Can Strengthen Cybersecurity in the Long Run

title: Why the CSU treats access to AI for students, staff as an equity issue
snippet: California State University's systemwide CIO argues that equitable AI access for students and staff prevents a digital divide and supports responsible AI literacy. Limiting access will not make AI disappear; it will simply deepen inequity and make it harder to teach AI's responsible use.

title: Public higher education: artificial intelligence usage (SCR 82)
snippet: The California Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted SCR 82, a resolution encouraging the University of California, California State University, and California Community Colleges to convene an intersegmental workgroup to discuss strategies and best practices for AI use in higher education.

title: Brown University Professor Criticizes School's Response to Massive AI Cheating Scandal
snippet: A Brown University economics professor sharply criticized the university's handling of a widespread AI cheating scandal in his spring 2026 course. The incident, where over 50 students allegedly used AI on a take-home exam, has sparked intense debate over academic integrity and AI detection.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This high-profile Ivy League incident highlights the severe challenges of managing academic integrity, take-home assessments, and AI detection policies at peer research universities.

title: UC System Halts Review of Standardized Testing in Admissions
snippet: While the board rescinded its original roadmap and timeline, it has not abandoned the broader review of admissions policies, according to UC Academic Senate Chair Ahmet Palazoglu. In a statement Monday, Palazoglu assured faculty, students, and the public that the Academic Senate is 'not rescinding its commitment to a comprehensive review of standardized testing in admissions.'
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Ahmet Palazoglu is quoted directly as the UC Academic Senate Chair regarding the review of admissions policies, which are being reevaluated in part due to the widespread adoption of AI-assisted application essays.

title: Brown University Generative AI in Teaching and Learning (GAITL) Committee Final Report and Recommendations
snippet: The GAITL Committee's final report outlines recommendations for Brown University to navigate generative AI in teaching and learning. title: Brown Professor Suspects Majority of His Class Used AI to Cheat
snippet: A Brown University professor voided a midterm exam and mandated an in-person final after suspecting widespread AI cheating, resulting in 19 failing grades. The incident has intensified national higher education debates over the unreliability of AI detection tools, the challenges of adjudicating large-scale academic integrity cases, and the urgent need for structural assessment redesign.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This high-profile peer-university incident highlights systemic vulnerabilities in traditional assessments and AI detection, directly informing UC's academic integrity policies.
